<local_ctx>
def insort : Nat → List Nat → List Nat
  | n, [] => [n]
  | n, x::xs => if n <= x then n::x::xs else x::(insort n xs)

def sorted : List Nat → Bool
  | [] => True
  | [_x] => True
  | x::y::xs => and (x <= y) (sorted (y::xs))
</local_ctx>

response:
<reasoning>
The goal after elaboration is `sorted xs = true → sorted (insort x xs) = true`, so `sorted xs` is a `Bool` and the hypothesis is an equality, not an implication. Thus `intro h` fails because there is no binder to introduce (Lean already has `h : sorted xs = true` in context). Also the `cases xs with` syntax was mis-indented; use `cases xs with | nil => ... | cons y ys => ...` at the same indentation level.
</reasoning>

<lean4_proof>
  cases xs with
  | nil =>
      simp [insort, sorted]
  | cons y ys =>
      intro h
      simpa using (lemma_hint_2 x y ys h)
</lean4_proof>
